channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: sports
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: sports

item.title: LeBron James sits out All-Star Game, ending 20-year streak of starts
item.description: SAN FRANCISCO (AP) — LeBron James wanted to play in yet another All-Star Game. His left foot and ankle didn't let him. The NBA's career scoring leader announced about 90 minutes before the start of the competition on Sunday that he couldn't play, citing ongoing injury issues.
item.dc:creator: TIM REYNOLDS, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: Vanderbilt's Mikayla Blakes scores NCAA Division I freshman record 55 points in win over Auburn
item.description: AUBURN, Ala. (AP) — Vanderbilt's Mikayla Blakes scored a Division I women's freshman record 55 points on Sunday in a 98-88 overtime victory over Auburn. Blakes, a 5-foot-8 guard who is averaging 21.8 points, shot 15 for 28 from the field with two 3-pointers and made 23 of 24 free throws.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Ludvig Aberg rallies down the stretch at Torrey Pines to win the Genesis Invitational
item.description: Ludvig Aberg left Torrey Pines feeling as bad as possible with a stomach bug that caused him to lose 10 pounds.
item.dc:creator: Doug Ferguson, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Azzi Fudd helps No. 7 UConn end No. 4 South Carolina's 71-game home win streak with 87-58 rout
item.description: COLUMBIA, S.C. (AP) — Azzi Fudd scored 28 points and No. 7 UConn ended No. 4 South Carolina's 71-game home winning streak with an 87-58 victory Sunday. Sarah Strong added 16 points and 13 rebounds for the Huskies (24-3), who had lost their past four to the Gamecocks.
item.dc:creator: PETE IACOBELLI, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Booker and No. 3 Texas rally from poor shooting start to beat No. 5 LSU 65-58
item.description: AUSTIN, Texas (AP) — Madison Booker scored 16 points, including four consecutive free throws in the final 24 seconds, and No. 3 Texas overcame a woeful shooting start to rally for a 65-58 victory over No. 5 LSU on Sunday.
item.dc:creator: JIM VERTUNO, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Ballard leads Wichita State to 84-79 OT win over No. 14 Memphis
item.description: WICHITA, Kan. (AP) — Quincy Ballard scored 14 of his 19 points in the second half and overtime to help Wichita State beat No. 14 Memphis 84-79 on Sunday. Bijan Cortes hit a 3 to give the Shockers a 78-77 lead with 1:10 remaining in OT. Wichita State never relinquished the lead from that point.
item.dc:creator: CODY FRIESEN, Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: Liverpool continues Premier League title charge and Man United's season keeps getting worse
item.description: Liverpool continued its charge toward the Premier League title on Sunday with a 2-1 win against Wolverhampton. For Manchester United, it just keeps getting worse after a 1-0 loss at Tottenham.
item.dc:creator: JAMES ROBSON, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: World Series champion Bobby Jenks battling stomach cancer
item.description: Bobby Jenks, a former Chicago White Sox and Boston Red Sox relief pitcher, revealed on Saturday he's battling stomach cancer and will undergo treatment.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Trump expected to attend NASCAR's Daytona 500 after Super Bowl LIX appearance
item.description: President Donald Trump is set to appear at the Daytona 500 on Sunday afternoon for the second time. He previously was at the 2020 "Great American Race.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: No. 11 Michigan State beats Illinois 79-65; Izzo passes Knight in Big Ten coaching victories
item.description: CHAMPAIGN, Ill. (AP) — Jaxson Kohler had 23 points and 10 rebounds, Tre Holloman scored 14 points and No. 11 Michigan State rallied to beat Illinois 79-65 on Saturday night giving the Spartans' Tom Izzo the most conference wins in Big Ten history.
item.dc:creator: STEVE STEIN, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Patrick Rodgers takes the lead at Torrey Pines as Scheffler and McIlroy falter
item.description: Rodgers was at his best Saturday when golf's best were faltering on a tough day at Torrey Pines, playing mistake-free until dumping a gap wedge into the water on the par-5 18th for his only bogey. That gave him a 4-under 68 and a one-shot lead going into the final round.
item.dc:creator: DOUG FERGUSON, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Kenley Jansen agrees to $10M, 1-year deal and Yoán Moncada agrees to $5M, 1-year deal with Angels
item.description: ANAHEIM, Calif. (AP) — The Los Angeles Angels agreed to a $10 million, one-year contract with veteran right-hander Kenley Jansen and a $5 million, one-year deal with veteran infielder Yoán Moncada on Saturday.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Former White Sox All-Star, World Series champion Bobby Jenks undergoing stomach cancer treatment
item.description: Bobby Jenks, a two-time All-Star closer and World Series champion with the Chicago White Sox, is being treated for stomach cancer. Jenks shared his condition from a Portugal hospital bed in an interview with MLB.com on Saturday, and the White Sox later sent their best wishes via social media.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Boys Basketball: Montgomery 58, Victory Christian Academy 44
item.description: SAN DIEGO (KUSI)- Boys hoops in Chula Vista on Tuesday night with Montgomery defeating Victory Christian Academy 58-44. Aztecs were lead by Senior JJ Sanchez. Montgomery was down at half and got going in the 2nd half for the double digit win.
item.dc:creator: Nick James, Fox 5

item.title: NHL exec hopes Canadians avoid 'unfortunate situation' of booing US national anthem ahead of rivalry game
item.description: Ahead of Saturday's U.S.-Canada matchup at the 4 Nations Face-Off, an NHL executive is hoping Canada's fans do not boo the U.S. national anthem again.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Ludvig Aberg drains first career hole-in-one during third round of Genesis Invitational
item.description: Ludvig Aberg drained his first career hole-in-one during the third round of the Genesis Invitational on Saturday, as he made it from 140 yards out.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Pirates ace Paul Skenes is looking to add to his already electric arsenal as he enters Year 2
item.description: BRADENTON, Fla. (AP) — Paul Skenes spent his rookie season dutifully following the Pittsburgh Pirates’ plan to bring their young ace along as slowly and safely as possible. It worked.
item.dc:creator: WILL GRAVES, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Dodgers star Shohei Ohtani throws 1st BP of spring training, taking a step toward possibly pitching
item.description: GLENDALE, Ariz. (AP) — Los Angeles Dodgers superstar Shohei Ohtani threw his first bullpen of spring training on Saturday, taking a step toward possibly pitching for the first time since 2023. The reigning World Series champion and National League MVP has not pitched in the big leagues since Aug.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Two-time All-Star Joey Gallo agrees to minor league contract with the White Sox
item.description: CHICAGO (AP) — Two-time All-Star Joey Gallo agreed to terms on a minor league contract with the Chicago White Sox on Thursday that includes a non-roster invite to spring training.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: Daytona 500: Married couple met at NASCAR's biggest race
item.description: "I wasn't looking for love, but love came and found me in the form of my beautiful wife," said Denny Lehmkuhl.
item.dc:creator: Xavier Walton, NewsNation

item.title: CMU Men's Wrestling "Battle in Brownson" recap
item.description: GRAND JUNCTION, Colo. (KREX) —Western Colorado Tops Colorado Mesa in ‘Battle in Brownson’ In a heated rivalry matchup inside Brownson Arena, the Colorado Mesa Mavericks faced off against the Western Colorado Mountaineers in the annual Battle in Brownson.
item.dc:creator: Titus Cleveland, KREX 5

item.title: A new professional soccer team might be coming to this Southern California city
item.description: A new stadium and entertainment district fit for a professional soccer team might be coming to Los Angeles County.
item.dc:creator: Travis Schlepp, KTLA 5

item.title: Study finds Bears fans most heartbroken in the NFL
item.description: CHICAGO (WGN) — In Chicago, sports and heartbreak go hand-in-hand. The Bears just went 5-12 when ahead of the season, they had playoff aspirations. The Bulls are fresh off back-to-back demolitions from the Detroit Pistons and embarking on a rebuild heading into the NBA all-star break.
item.dc:creator: Eli Ong, WGN 9

item.title: Early look: The views and venues of the 2026 Winter Olympics
item.description: As the countdown to the 2026 Winter Games continues, preparations are in full swing in Milan and Cortina.
item.dc:creator: Alyssa Orange, NewsNation

item.title: White Sox sign veteran CF Michael A. Taylor to 1-year deal
item.description: CHICAGO (AP) — Center fielder Michael A. Taylor and the Chicago White Sox have agreed to a $1.95 million, one-year contract. Chicago announced the agreement Wednesday. Taylor spent last season with the Pittsburgh Pirates, batting .193 with five home runs, 21 RBI and a .543 OPS in 113 games.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: It's a busy week in Colorado Mesa Maverick country
item.description:
Men’s Basketball. Women’s Basketball. Men’s Wrestling. Baseball. Softball. Swimming. Men’s Lacrosse. Women’s Lacrosse.
All eight of those teams are in action this week.
item.dc:creator: Big Will Levenson, KREX 5

item.title: Madsen scores 28, but Utes lose at Cincinnati
item.description: CINCINNATI (ABC4 Sports) - Despite 28 points from Gabe Madsen against his former team, the Runnin' Utes fell at Cincinnati Tuesday night, 85-75. Madsen, who began his college career with the Bearcats, made eight three-pointers on 22 attempts, in matching a season high point total.
item.dc:creator: Dana Greene, ABC 4

item.title: Clayton Kershaw to return to Los Angeles Dodgers: reports
item.description: Los Angeles Dodgers legend Clayton Kershaw is returning to the defending World Series champions for the 2025 season.
item.dc:creator: Travis Schlepp, KTLA 5

item.title: Cubs spring training: Shota Imanaga ready to help anchor starting rotation after spectacular rookie season
item.description: MESA, Ariz. — Old Man Winter has its icy grip on Chicagoland this week, but here's a warm reminder that summer is inching closer: Cubs pitchers and catchers reported to camp Monday.
item.dc:creator: Michael Johnson, WGN 9

item.title: Super Bowl losers get this ring instead
item.description: What about the losers? They also get a ring – though not one to commemorate their presence at the Super Bowl.
item.dc:creator: Alix Martichoux, NewsNation
